people.name: Wolphert Gerritsz van Couwenhoven
people.name_variations: Wolphert Gerritsen van Kouwenhoven; Wolfert Gerritsz; Wolphert Gerretse; Wolfert Gerritse; Wolphert Gerretse Van Kouwenhoven
people.birth_place: Amersfoort, Utrecht, Netherlands
people.death_place: New Amersfoort (Flatlands), Kings County, New York
people.residence_primary: AMERSFOORT, UTRECHT, NETHERLANDS (1611-1623): house on the Langegraft / Lievevrouwestraet from 30 Jan 1617 (purchased from Aert van Schayck), residence near the Coppelpoort (per Kemp 1995). POST-1625 (New Netherland): New Amersfoort (later Flatlands), western Long Island — his palisaded house was the focal point of the settlement
people.occupation: PRE-EMIGRATION (Amersfoort, Utrecht, Netherlands, 1611-1623): baker by 1611, then bleacher / co-owner of bleach camps outside the Coppelpoort, Amersfoort (per Kemp 1995). POST-1625 (New Netherland): Patentee; director of bouweries (farms); founder of New Amersfoort; Schepen of New Amsterdam (1654)
people.spouse_text: Neeltje Jacobsdochter — m. ca. 1609 Amersfoort
people.children_summary: Three sons emigrated with him to New Netherland April 1625: Gerrit (b. ca. 1610); Jacob (b. abt. 1615); Pieter (b. 1614). Possible additional daughters not yet researched.
people.sources: Wikipedia: Wolfert Gerritse van Couwenhoven — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wolfert_Gerritse_van_Couwenhoven | Cocheu, NYG&BR 70:3 (July 1939), pp. 230-235 | Kouwenhoven (1997-1998), NYG&BR 128:4 + 129:1 + 129:2
people.church_affiliations: No documented Reformed Church baptism for Wolphert nor any of his children (Kouwenhoven 1997 fn 51) — likely not Gereformeerd by birth. The 1605-01-17 Amersfoort Reformed marriage entry attributed to him by L.P. de Boer (1912) is REJECTED — refers to a different Wolfert Gerritse + Aeltgen Jansdochter (see feedback_1605_amersfoort_entry_different_couple.md). Marriage to Neeltgen Jacobsdr likely solemnized before 1611-12-15 but church record not located. Civic-rank marker: 1657-04-18 admitted to Small Burgher right of New Amsterdam (CNYHS 1885:24) — the Small Burgher right was a civic privilege, not a church affiliation, but membership in the Reformed Church was the social default for residents of New Amsterdam at this date.

people.name: Neeltje Jacobsdochter
people.name_variations: Neeltgen Jacobsdochter; Neeltje Jacobs; Neeltje Janse Jacobsdochter
people.birth_place: likely Amersfoort area, Utrecht, Netherlands
people.death_place: presumed New Amersfoort (Flatlands), Kings County, New York
people.residence_primary: New Amersfoort (later Flatlands), western Long Island
people.father_text: Jacob Petersz (deceased before 1611-12-15, Amersfoort) — per Kemp 1995, Amersfoort Notarieel Archief AT002b002 f. 282.
people.mother_text: Metgen Jacobsdr (deceased before 1611-12-15, Amersfoort) — per Kemp 1995, Amersfoort Notarieel Archief AT002b002 f. 282.
people.spouse_text: Wolphert Gerritsz van Couwenhoven — m. ca. 1609 Amersfoort
people.children_summary: Three sons emigrated April 1625: Gerrit, Jacob, Pieter. notes re a putative "Jan" who is a grandson, not a son.
people.church_affiliations: No documented Reformed Church baptism for Neeltgen (Kouwenhoven 1997 fn 51). Marriage to Wolphert solemnized before 1611-12-15; specific church not located. Brother Herman Jacobsz (Van Es) married before the Leiden schepens 1607 (NOT in the Reformed Church) — suggesting Neeltgen's natal family may not have been Gereformeerd by birth either.

people.name: Gerrit Wolphertsen van Couwenhoven
people.name_variations: Gerret Wolphertsen van Couwenhoven; Gerret Wolfertse van Couwenhoven; Garret Wolfertse van Couwenhoven; Gerret Wolphertsen; Gerret Wolfersen
people.birth_place: Amersfoort, Utrecht, Netherlands
people.marriage_place: Flatlands (Midwout), Kings County, New York — also reported as Oyster Bay, Nassau, New York; sources differ
people.residence_primary: Midwout (Flatbush), Kings County, New York — settled on 50 morgens of land with deeds dated 1638-01-26 and 1641-09-16
people.occupation: Settler/landholder; in 1643 a member of a delegation sent to the States General in Holland to petition regarding the forlorn and defenseless condition of New Netherlands under Director Willem Kieft's war with the Indians
people.spouse_text: Aeltje Cornelis Cool (WikiTree Cool-29, abt.1615-1683) — m. 1635, Flatlands/Oyster Bay; after Gerrit's death she remarried Elbert Elbertse Stoothoff on 1645-08-27
people.children_summary: Four children with Aeltje Cornelis Cool: Willem Gerritsz (WikiTree Couwenhoven-4, b. 1636 Flatlands); Jan Gerretse (WikiTree Van_Couwenhoven-39, abt.1639-abt.1724); Neeltje Gerrits Schenck (WikiTree Van_Couwenhoven-29, 1641-abt.1672); Marretje Gerretse Van Voorhees (bp. 1644-04-10 RDC Manhattan, d. 1702-1709).
people.sources: Cocheu, NYG&BR 70:3 (July 1939), pp. 230-235 | Kouwenhoven NYG&BR 129:2 (Apr 1998), pp. 109-120 — Table 2 confirms birth ca. 1610 + 3-son family list | Cocheu NYG&BR 70:4 (Oct 1939), pp. 353-356
people.church_affiliations: Presumed DRC New Amsterdam (childhood) → DRC Flatbush / Flatlands (adulthood — daughter Marretje bp. 1644-04-10 New Amsterdam BDC 17, suggesting family attended NA DRC in mid-1640s before settling Flatlands). No specific membership records cited by Cocheu 70.3 / 70.4. Per Cocheu 70.4: 'made his mark to documents' (semi-literate, like his father).

people.children_summary: At least two children: Neeltje Jacobse Wolphertz (bp. 1639-09-25); Johannes Jacobs "Johanes" Van Cowenhoven (b. ca. 1641; WikiTree Kouwenhoven-31) — canonical per Cocheu, "The Couenhovens and Kinsfolk" (FamilySearch item 284005), which traces descent through "Jacob Wolphertsen van Couwenhoven and his first wife Hester Jansen". Likely also Petronel Jacobs Van Vleck (whose row is listed on Jacob's WikiTree children list).

people.name: Pieter Wolphertsen van Couwenhoven

people.birth_place: Amersfoort, Utrecht, Netherlands

people.death_place: New Amsterdam / Brooklyn, Kings County, New York
people.residence_primary: New Amsterdam / Brooklyn, Kings County, New York
people.occupation: Dutch brewer (New Amsterdam); landholder (Brooklyn / New Castle, DE); reportedly "Lieutenant" in colonial militia per Geni secondary source "Lt Pieter Wolphertse van Couwenhoven" (rank requires direct verification from primary sources)
people.spouse_text: 1st: Hester (Symons) Sijmons / "Daws" — m. 1640-12-02 New Amsterdam (she was widow of Jaques de Vernuis, from Amsterdam); 2nd: Aeltje Sybrants (WikiTree Sybrants-2, 1630-1689) — m. 1665-11-22 Reformed Dutch Church of New Amsterdam. Extramarital: Maria du Trieux (WikiTree Du_Trieux-45) bore his acknowledged daughter Aeltje du Trieux Pieters per 1642-01-07 adoption document in the Dutch Manuscripts of New Amsterdam. | (3) UNCONFIRMED — Cocheu 70:4 p. 355 reports 'some historians state that he m. (3) May 19, 1699, Josynthe Thomas'; not in MDC, no primary citation given; flag for further research, do NOT add to W related_family.
people.children_summary: Seven children per Wolfertszen-1 WikiTree list: with Maria du Trieux (extramarital) — Aeltje (du Trieux) Pieters, b. bef. 1642-01-07, acknowledged/adopted by Pieter; with Hester Simons (1st wife, INFERRED) — Petrus #1 (likely died young), Cornelius, Hester, Annetje (m. Anders Olafsson Stille by 1671), Aeltje Couwenhoven; with Aeltje Sybrants (2nd wife) — Petrus #2 (WikiTree Couwenhoven-72, b. 1665, bp. 1669-02-27 NYC RDC, d. 1705).
people.sources: Kouwenhoven NYG&BR 128:4 (Oct 1997), pp. 209-218 — 1640 New Amsterdam marriage record citing Pieter as bachelor 'from Amersfoort' | Cocheu NYG&BR 70:4 (Oct 1939), pp. 353-356
people.church_affiliations: Reformed Dutch Church of New Amsterdam (1640-12-02 marriage to Hester Symons, listed as 'j.m. from Amersfoort' in MDC 10); Schepen of New Amsterdam 1653+ — civic-rank role implies Reformed Church communicancy at the period; 1665-11-22 m.(2) Aeltje Sibrants at RDC NY (MDC 31).

Gerret, b. 1662-01-04, bp. 1662-01-15 (Brkn 146); m. Aeltie — (KCo 77). By 2nd marriage to Jannetje Pieterse Monfoort (m. 1665-02-12): 20. Altie, b. 1665-12-14, d. abt. 1689; m. 1687-03-16 Cornelis Symonse Van Arsdalen. 21. Neeltje, b. 1669-02-07; m. 1692 John Pieterse Wyckoff. 22. Pieter, b. 1671-02-12, d. abt. Feb 1755; m. abt. 1694 Patience Daws. 23. Cornelis, b. 1672-11-20, d. 1736-05-16; m. 1700-09-03 Margarieta Schenck (cousin's #43). 24. Sara, b. 1674-12-20, d. 1761-01-31; m. 1692-10-01 Jan Roelofse Schenck (cousin's #41). 25. Albert, b. 1676-12-07, d. 1748-09-13; m. 1701-10-02 Neeltje Schenck (cousin's #44). 26. Jacob, b. 1679-01-29, bp. 1679-02-02 (Flat 111); m. 1705-11-12 Sarah Schenck (cousin's #46). 27. Jan (existing John Willemse Couwenhoven), b. 1681-04-09, bp. 1681-04-11 (Flat 120); m. abt. 1704 Coba Vanderveer. 28. Annetje, b. 1683-04-13, bp. 1683-04-22 (Flat 128); m.(1 sup.) 1704-11-11 Aert Willemse; m.(2 sup.) Johannes Antonides. 29. William (existing Willem Willemse Kowenhoven), b. 1686-03-07, d. 1769-01-19; m. 1709-06-05 Annetie Lucasse Van Voorhees. 30. Jacomina, b. 1689-12-28; m. 1709-06-05 Elbert Willemsen. Bergen (KCo 80) suggests possible 13th child Joris — Cocheu places him as #92 (next gen).
people.sources: Cocheu NYG&BR 71:1 (Jan 1940), pp. 69-74 | Stiles, A History of the City of Brooklyn, vol. 1 (1867): p. 425 [founding member, First Reformed Dutch Church of Brooklyn, 12 Mar 1660]; p. 429 [elected Deacon, 1663 & 1664]. Transcribed in project Bibliography (Stiles entries) 2026-06-08

Marike Schenck, b. 1667-02-14; m. 1687-02-15 Isaac Hegeman. 41. Jan Roelofse Schenck, b. 1670-03-01, d. 1753-01-30; m. Flatlands 1692-10-01 his cousin Sara Willemse Cowenhoven ('s #24). 42. Gerret Roelofse Schenck, b. 1671-10-27, d. 1745-09-05; m. Flatlands abt. 1693 his cousin Neeltje Coertse Van Voorhees ('s #51). By Schenck's 2nd marriage to Annetje Wyckoff: 43. Margaretta (m. cousin Cornelis Willemse Cowenhoven's #23); 44. Neeltje (m. cousin Albert Willemse Cowenhoven's #25); 45. Mayke; 46. Sara (m. cousin Jacob Willemse Cowenhoven's #26). [Schenck 2nd-marriage children noted for cousin-marriage tracking only.]

people.name: Gerret Willemse Couwenhoven
people.birth_place: Brooklyn, Kings Co., NY
people.spouse_text: Two marriages per Cocheu 1989 A&C p.17 (Mrs. John Spell): 1st wife name unknown (m. 1690 per Flatbush DRC fee book — Frost Coll. v.1 p.28); 2nd wife Lysbeth ——— surname unknown (m. 1692 per Flatbush DRC fee book — Frost Coll. v.1 p.29; named on baptismal entries 1699-1721 as mother of his children; still living 1721 at a grandchild's baptism). Earlier 'Aeltie' attribution (Cocheu 71:1 / Bergen KCo 77) was Bergen's conflation with cousin Gerret Jans⁴ #31 — A&C ruled out via Flatbush DRC fee-book chronology (Gerret Jans⁴ not yet of marriageable age in 1690 or 1692). Crowd-sourced surname candidates for Lysbeth: 'Hoos' (FindaGrave 173362300); 'Andut' (FamilySearch GDT6-282).
people.children_summary: Per Cocheu 1989 A&C p.17 (Mrs. John Spell): 6 children by 2nd wife Lysbeth, retaining Cocheu's original entry numbering under #31. The earlier Cocheu 71:1 statement 'no record of any children for them has been found' is SUPERSEDED by this correction. Children: (#201) Willem⁵ b. abt. 1693; m. Ariantje Bennet, dau. Willem Willemse Bennet × Ariantje Van de Water (Record v.94, p.156) —. (#92) Joris⁵, b. abt. 1695; m. 1719-05-26 Flatbush DRC Aletta Luyster. Unnamed child b. abt. 1697, d. infancy (Census, Doc.Hist.NY v.3 p.133) —. (#197) Jacomyntje⁵ bp. Brooklyn 1699-01-15, d. infancy —. (#198) Jacomyntje⁵ bp. Brooklyn 1701-03-02; m. 1720-09-17 Elbert Luyster, brother of Joris's wife Aletta Luyster (Frost Coll. Fbush Marr. v.1 p.61 — double Cowenhoven-Luyster sibling-marriage) —. (#199) Aeltje⁵ bp. Brooklyn 1701-03-02 (twin of #198); no further record —.
people.sources: Cocheu NYG&BR 71:1 (Jan 1940), pp. 69-74 | Cocheu NYG&BR 71:2 (Apr 1940), pp. 157-162

Original: New Jersey State Archives. New Jersey, Published Archives Series, First Series, Volume XXX, Abstracts of Wills, 1730-1750. Trenton, NJ: John L. Murphy Publishing Company. Will of Cornelius Couwenhoven (Covenhoven), Middletown Township, Monmouth Co., yeoman, dated 1735-11-22; proved 1736-06-22. Lib. C, p. 107. Ancestry collection: https://www.ancestry.com/search/collections/2793/; existing Active rows 87-91 (-096) source: https://www.ancestry.com/search/collections/2793/records/16187 (Hannah, indexed record)
people.notable_records: 1735-11-22 will (Lib. C, p. 107; proved 1736-06-22) bequeathing 5 enslaved people: Hannah ("the old negro woman") → wife Margaret with all estate; Sam and Tom → eldest son William (= Willem Cornelis Couwenhoven, Couwenhove-3, 1700-1755), with 156 acres from William Bowne (1704+1705) + 120 acres from Daniel Hendricks/John Schenck/Garrat Schenck/Peter Weycoff (1716); James and Toney → son Roeloffe (= Roelof Cornelisse Couwenhoven), with residue of land + meadow + 2½ acres from James and Rachel Hubbard (1706) + 2 acres from Jonathan Holmes (1719) + 10 acres from John Bowne (1703) + 125 acres from Garrat and John Schenck (1700). Executors: wife Margaret + sons William and Roeloffe. Witnesses: Gerret Schenck (likely brother-in-law via wife's Schenck family), Johannis Bennet, Robert Dodsworth. EVENT.
people.will_probate: Will dated Nov. 22, 1735; pro. June 22, 1736 (NJWills, 2:115; Lib. C, p. 107). EVENT. Per Cocheu #23 p.19.

Du. Ref. Ch. NY 1749-03-17 (DDC 174).
people.children_summary: Natural (illegitimate) son Samuel Cowenhoven (main heir; father of Frans's grandchildren Edward and Mary). John Hendrickson).

New Jersey, Published Archives Series, First Series, Volume XXX, Abstracts of Wills, 1730-1750. Trenton, NJ: John L. Murphy Publishing Company. Will of Jacob Covenhoven of Middletown, Monmouth Co., yeoman, dated 1743-07-05; proved 1744-07-17. Lib. D, p. 152. Inventory taken 1744-12-03 (£618.18.8) by Samuel Ogborne, Hendrick Hendrickson and Edward Taylor — includes "six negros, large Bible, 217 bushels Indian corn, books, 32 barrels flour" plus extensive bonds and book debts
people.notable_records: 1743-07-05 will (Lib. D, p. 152, Middletown Township, Monmouth Co., yeoman); proved 1744-07-17. 1744-12-03 inventory (£618.18.8) lists "six negros, large Bible, 217 bushels Indian corn, books, 32 barrels flour" plus extensive bonds and book debts from many neighbors (including William Covenhoven of Flat Lands NY and Rulof + Jacob Covenhoven, likely his own sons). Inventory enslaved-people group is UNNAMED —. Executors: 3 sons Roeloff + Jacob + Garret. Witnesses: Joseph Goulder, George Crawford, Elias Golden, Charles Henderson. Inventory takers: Samuel Ogborne, Hendrick Hendrickson, Edward Taylor.

Previously hypothesized; now confirmed. With Jacobus Cosine (2nd husband): at least Cornelius Cozyn and Gerritt Cozyn (inferred from their being named John Cowenhoven's brothers in the 1753 will; both alive 1753, named co-executors). Children with Jacobus Cosine NOT yet added to FAMILY — deferred for later pass.

people.burial_place: Old Brick Reformed Church Cemetery, Marlboro, Monmouth Co., NJ (per FindaGrave memorial 173362300; specific grave location lost per FG mass-burial caveat)

people.father_text: Gerret Willemse⁴ Van Kouwenhoven. MATCH per Cocheu 1989 A&C addition by Mrs. John Spell, p.17 (lines 668, 674, 688 of canonical.md): Joris explicitly listed as son of Gerret Willemse⁴ and his 2nd wife Lysbeth, retaining Cocheu's original #92 numbering. Documentary anchor: Flatbush DRC fee book — 6 g. paid by 'Gerret Couwenhoven, bridegroom' in 1690 (1st marriage) and 1692 (2nd marriage to Lysbeth) per Frost Coll. Fbush Marr. v.1, pp.28-29. Cousin Gerret Jans⁴ #31 was not yet of marriageable age in either year, ruling out Bergen's earlier conflation that placed an 'Aeltie' with. Earlier 'son of Willem³ by 1st wife Aeltje Brinckerhoff' theory (Bergen + Honeyman + Schenck Conover pre-1902) retracted by L.H. Schenck Conover's 1902 letter as 'an error' he could not prove.
people.mother_text: Lysbeth ——— (surname not recorded by Cocheu). 2nd wife of Gerret Willemse, m. ~1692 Flatbush per DRC fee book. Named on baptismal entries 1699-1721 as mother of his children, per Cocheu 1989 A&C p.17; still living with husband in 1721 at a grandchild's baptism. Crowd-sourced surname candidates: 'Hoos' per FindaGrave 173362300; 'Andut' per FamilySearch LKDN-6QN. Naming-pattern signal supporting attribution: Joris named his daughter Elizabeth = Lysbeth equivalent — fits Dutch maternal-grandmother second-daughter naming rule.
people.spouse_text: Altje Luyster, dau. of Cornelis Pieterse Luyster + Sarah Catharina Nevius; b. ~1695 Flatbush. Sources per Cocheu 81:2 #92: HSYB 1898 p.99; Nevius Gen. 1900 p.593. m. Flatbush DRC 1719-05-26.
people.children_summary: 7 children per Cocheu 81:2 #315-321: (315) Sarah6 b. abt.1720 d. 1812 — m. 1743-05-17 Middletown NJ Aaron Lane (a Schenck-line cousin via #226 Antje Schenck + Mathias Lane); 10+ Lane children listed; family relocated 1746 to Readington Hunterdon Co. NJ; (316) Gerrit6 b. abt.1722-23 — m. Sarah Traphagen (Ruloff + Cornelia Putnam Traphagen's dau., bp.1729-06-19); 8 children incl. David d.1800 bur. Old Churchyard Whitehouse NJ; (317) Elizabeth6 b. 1725-03-18 d. 1812-05-07 — m. 1747-05-07 John Smock (a Schenck-line cousin via #228 Mary Schenck + Henry Smock); 13 Smock children with extensive Cowenhoven cousin marriages; (318) Cornelius6 bp. 1727-07-02 Middletown d. 1805-10-01 ae.78 bur. Old Churchyard Whitehouse NJ — m. 1756-06-05 Helena Williamson (dau. William Williamson + Aeltje Couwenhoven — Cowenhoven cousin marriage via Albert line); 1 daughter Anne; (319) Unnamed Daughter6 bp. 1729-01-01 — no further info; (320) Peter6 b. 1731-02-26 d. 1801-02-03 — m. 1752-02-06 Eleanor Polhemus); 10 children; will 1800-11-15 (NJA 39:103); (321) Joris6 bp. 1734-03-31 Middletown — m. Margaret —; 2 daughters; will 1772-05-25 (NJA 34:114) bur. Old Churchyard Whitehouse NJ.

people.notable_records: 1738-03-19 ADULT CHURCH ADMISSION of Yana at Old Tennent Reformed Church, Manalapan/Freehold area, Monmouth Co. NJ — recorded as "baptism" but per Dutch Reformed tradition this is admission to church membership and Yana would have been an adult (b. ~1716-1722). 1747-03-08 simultaneous ADULT CHURCH ADMISSION of three of Yana's children — Mack, Cyro, Nancy — at the same Old Tennent Church (b. ~1725-1735, consistent with Yana having children in her late teens/early twenties). 1786-11-18 will (Lib. 28, p. 296; NJ Abstract of Wills Vol XXXVI p.56), proved 1790-03-19 (the day testator died, matching WikiTree d.1790-03-19). Bequests: Son Benjamin (£400 bond + farming utensils share); Son David (deceased — his sons John, David, Garret get the place where son William lives, must pay William £200); Son Joseph (150 acres of land + farming utensils); Son Isaac (use of place he lives on, then sold; his son Thomas £100; his daughters Sarah/Elinor/Elizabeth £50 each; remainder to rest of Isaac's children); Son William Jr. ("farm I live on" + farming utensils + executor); Son Albert (likely deceased — only mentioned via "grandson Cornelius son of Albert" £5); Daughters Hendrika, Ellinor, Elizabeth (goods and livestock); Daughter Mary (deceased — her children get goods and livestock share). NO WIFE MENTIONED — Lijsbet Van Cleef predeceased him. Executors: son William Jr., Garret Vanderveer, John Craig (merchant). Witnesses: Alexander Low, Stephen Heavland, John Craig. 1790-03-17 inventory £183.9.2 (Lib. 30, p. 400) by Joseph Clayton, James Tapscott, Samuel P. Forman — abstract gives total only, no item breakdown; would need direct consultation of full inventory to see if Yana et al. are listed. NO INDIVIDUAL ENSLAVED PEOPLE NAMED IN THE WILL — likely under "goods and livestock" residuary OR disposed of before 1786 OR deceased by 1786 (Yana would be ~64-70; children ~51-65).

people.children_summary: 7 children per Cocheu #214 p.83 (prob. all b. Leffert Lefferts); Dinah⁶ b.1741-11-15 d.1827-05-20 (m. Robert Benson; FG-variant dates 1762/1847 flagged REVIEW); Nicholas⁶ "Judge" b.1744 d.1793-03-07 (m. Jannetje Lott); Catharine⁶ b.1749 d.pre-1819 (Cocheu #655, m. Catharine Clopper; NB also styled "Major John" in his 1805 Brooklyn death notice [Cocheu #656] — DISTINCT from Major John of New Utrecht).
people.sources: Stiles, A History of the City of Brooklyn, vol. 1 (1867): p. 251 fn 1 [owner of 'Cowenhoven's boschje'/Fort Putnam hill, 'John Cowenhoven, sen.']; Map of Bedford Corners (foldout btwn pp. 266-267) large fig. #23 [John Cowenhoven, Bedford farm; attribution tentative]. Vol. 2 (1869): p. 169 [the 'old John Cowenhoven house,' W side of Fort Greene Place ~160 ft N of Atlantic]. Transcribed in project Bibliography (Stiles entries) 2026-06-08
people.notable_records: 1778-06-01 Probate Inventory (Town of Brooklyn) lists 5 enslaved people held at his death — Frederick, Tom, Ben, Anthony, Mary; inventory survives in the John R. Cowenhoven Papers at NYHS, indicating the family papers descended through grandson John R. Couwenhoven (Rem's son, Nicholas R.'s brother — stub). | EVENT:; SOURCE:. | NEW research target 2026-04-28:'s Last Will and Testament dated 28 August 1775 — referenced (whereas-clause) in the 1 May 1789 woodland-division indenture (Brooklyn Cowenhoven Land Agreements pp.421-424). The will's 7th devise gave 'Woodland not particularly devised' jointly to sons Rem and John, establishing the joint inheritance that drove the 1789 division between the Rem-sons and uncle John. Primary will not yet located; same archival series as (NYHS John R. Cowenhoven Papers) is the place to look first.
people.will_probate: Will dated Aug. 28, 1775; proved May 29, 1778 (WNYHS 9:33-35). Witnesses: Johannes Vanderveer, Simon Lott, Engelbert Lott. Will named sons Rem, Nicholas, John; daughters Dorothea (m. Leffert Lefferts), Catharine (m. Abraham Luqueer), Elsie, Dinah; wife mentioned but not by name. Per 1789 woodland-division indenture (Brooklyn Cowenhoven Land Agreements pp.421-424), the 7th devise gave joint woodland to sons Rem and John. Primary will not yet located; same NYHS archival series is first place to look.

unnamed_groups.record_type: colonial census (1755 Census of Slaves; counts by sex only, no ages, no names in this return)
unnamed_groups.grouping_language: 1755 Census of Slaves return for John Cowenhoven: "4 male ... 1 female." The 1755 census was taken under an act of assembly counting slaves '14 years and upwards' (so stated on the Flatlands return); the Capt. John Lott (Brooklyn) return does not restate the age limit. No ages given per-person; adult_m=4 / adult_f=1 records SEX (and probable age 14+), not certainty of adulthood.
unnamed_groups.source_citation: cp_sou_093

people.spouse_text: Jannetje Hendrickson — m. 1741-08-12 Freehold-Middletown Dutch Reformed Church (Cocheu 82:2 #161). After Roelof's death, Jannetje m.(2) 1747 Peter Schenck (cousin, son of + Jan Roelofse Schenck = #145), and had 6 Schenck children (per Cocheu 82:2). Widow declined administration 1746-12-20.
people.children_summary: 3 children (per Cocheu 82:2 #161): (1) Sarah⁶ bap. 1742-02-21 FM, m. 1763-07-11 Benjamin Van Cleef b. 1742-09-19 (son of Benjamin Van Cleef + Elinor Covenhoven/#441); (2) Daniel⁶ bap. 1744-01-15, d. 1821-02-18 probably unmarried; (3) Catrina⁶ bap. 1746-02-16, d. 1811-11-21, m. 1765-02-28 David Vanderveer b. 1748-08-02 d. 1810 (son of Teunis + Aeltje Gerretse Schenck Vanderveer) — 6 children including Roelof (m. Nelly Lloyd, daughter of Sarah Covenhoven #508).
people.sources: Cocheu, Lincoln C. "The Van Kouwenhoven-Conover Family." NYG&BR vol. LXXXII, no. 2 (April 1951): #161. Earlier reference: Cocheu NYG&BR 71:2 #161. Original probate: New Jersey, Published Archives Series, First Series, Volume XXX, Abstracts of Wills, 1730-1750, p.116; Trenton, NJ State Archives
people.notable_records: 1746-10-24 intestate administration grant (NJA:30:116; admin lib. D, p. 420 Monmouth Co.) — Administrators: Peter Covenhoven (likely brother = #165), William Hendrickson (likely brother-in-law), Teunis De Nyse; bondsman Hendrick Hendrickson. Inventory £439:12:4¾ taken Nov 17, 1746 by Nicholas Johnson, Elias Golden, John Taylor. Widow Jannetje declined to administer 1746-12-20 (witnesses Cornelius and Mary Lane).

people.name: John (Jan) Cowenhoven
people.name_variations: Jan (as in father's 1711 will); John Cowenhoven (as in own 1753 will); Jan Van Couwenhoven; John Cowenhoven of Bushwick

people.residence_primary: Bushwick, Kings County, New York (inherited from father Gerret Janse per 1711 will)
people.occupation: Carpenter (per own 1753 will self-identification — cf. father Gerret's 1711 will which named him heir but not occupation)
people.mother_text: Aaltje van der Wijk — CONFIRMED 2026-04-24 via own 1753 will evidence: testator names "brother Cornelius Cozyn" and "brother Gerritt Cozyn" as executors, which only makes sense as half-brothers via his mother's 2nd marriage to Jacobus Cosine/Cozyn (Aaltje remarried Jacobus Cosine bef. 1712-11-03 Gerret's 1st wife Lysbet predeceased him and cannot have had post-1712 children).
people.spouse_text: 1st: — (unnamed, possibly d.y.); 2nd: Maria Cozine (per Cocheu 71:3 #200)
people.children_summary: Per own 1753 will: 9 children combined from two marriages — Pauline, Gerritt, Altie, Gabriel, John/Jan, Gertruy, Essye, Marya, Annatie. Specific bequests: Gerritt (£5 from £100 Cornelius Cosine debt + £20 + silver beaker + best horse), Jan (£15), Altie (20 shillings). Residual split among all 9 children.
people.sources: NY Surrogate's Office Liber 8 p.275 — named 'my son John' in father Gerret's 1711 will (heir, with Aeltie, to all real and personal estate). No; NY Surrogate's Office Liber 21 p.96 — own will of John Cowenhoven of Bushwick, carpenter, dated 1753-02-01, proved 1761-08-17 (FamilySearch book 167253 pp.108-109: | Cocheu NYG&BR 71:3 (Jul 1940), pp. 273-276

people.spouse_text: 1st: Sarah — (surname unknown; d. 1756-08-20 per Cocheu 82:4 #215). 2nd: Antje Bergen (bap. 1710-03-12 New York Dutch Church BDC:344; daughter of Hans & Sarah Rapelye Bergen) - m. after 1756.
people.children_summary: Per 1781 will: Son Johannes Couwenhoven (alive 1781); Son Necasie Couwenhoven (dec'd by 1781, leaving widow Nieltie + 6 children: granddaughters Antie, Geertie, Sarah, Elizabeth, Nieltie + grandson Garret); Daughter Sarah Couwenhoven (m. Martin Schenck).
people.sources: New York, U.S., Wills and Probate Records 1659-1999 — Garret Couwenhoven 1781 will, Vol 0035-0039 pp.222-225 (Ancestry.com 2015; NY Surrogate's Court NY County); FAMILY cross-reference for full biographical data. Cocheu 82:4 #215 - primary identification + 1781 will text WNYHS:12:169-71
people.notable_records: 1781-05-09 will (probated 1784-02-05 by Thomas Tredwell, NY Surrogate's Court) — bequeaths "All my Negroes together with all my household furniture" divided 1/3 to son Johannes, 1/3 to daughter Sarah Schenck, 1/3 to children of dec'd son Necasie. Total enslaved count UNKNOWN (no individual names, no inventory in this entry). Witnessed by Petrus Van Pelt, John Verkerk Van Nuys, Nichs. Couwenhoven.
people.will_probate: Will dated 1781-05-09 / proved 1784-02-05 (WNYHS:12:169-71). Names son Johannes; daughter Sarah wife of Martin Schenck; daughter-in-law Neeltje (Nelly Van Pelt) widow of deceased son Nicholas (spouse, d. 1778-10-18); grandchildren Garret, Antje, Geertje, Sarah, Elizabeth, Neeltje Covenhoven. Executors: son Johannes + friend Aert Van Pelt son of Petrus + daughter Sarah Covenhoven. Witnesses: Petrus Van Pelt, John Van Kirk Van Nuys yeoman, Nicholas Covenhoven Esquire.

people.children_summary: 6 children per Cocheu 83:2 #677-682; will mentions Jacob, Garret, Peter, Rachel: (677) Jacob⁶ bap. 1768-10-30 Schraalenburg, d. 1848-11-04 Leonia NJ, m. 1796-05-17 Aletta Westervelt — Jacob Cowenhoven of Liberty Pole / Englewood NJ (commonly confused with Jacobus⁷ of Westchester); (678) Hendrick; (other children TBD via Cocheu 83:2 pull). [Full list to enrich from Cocheu 83:2 in subsequent commit.]
people.sources: Cocheu NYG&BR 71:4 #264 — primary; Cocheu 83:2 #677-682 — children list and Berdan-spouse confirmation; Black Loyalist Directory Book 2 p. 151 — posthumous identification by Jack
people.notable_records: Cocheu NYG&BR 71:4 #264 — primary genealogical attestation; Cocheu 83:2 #677-682 — children list and Berdan spouse confirmation; 1781-11-20 Will (NJA:35:238) — proved 1783-06-27; 1783-09-22 — posthumous identification as former enslaver in Black Loyalist Directory Book 2 p. 151 by Jack, who registered for evacuation to Annapolis Royal aboard ship LORD TOWNSHEND.
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Jack, registered in BLD Book 2 p. 151 on 1783-09-22 as formerly enslaved by Samuel of Hackensack; age 60, "stout fellow"; embarked ship LORD TOWNSHEND bound for Annapolis Royal, Nova Scotia).

people.notable_records: bequest names wife Mary; son Jacob (residuary legatee, 'Jacob the miller of Freehold'); daughters Nelly Lane, Sarah Smock, Caty Lane (all married daughters). Executors: brother-in-law Garret Hendrickson + friend Jacob Covenhoven. Witnesses: Simon Van Arsdale, Isaac Van Pelt, Cornelius Van Horn, Jacob Van Arsdale, John Schenck Jr., Peter Van Doren. taken 1792-06-07 by John Stillwell + Isaac Covenhoven. Cocheu textual note: 'The omission of the names of Arie, John, and the younger Antje leads to the belief that these children had predeceased their father leaving no issue.'.
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Betty, 1792 will bequest — MATCH per 9-source Cocheu #166 ↔ convergence 2026-05-28)
people.will_probate: Will dated 1792-02-01, proved 1792-06-08 by Monmouth Surrogate (recorded NJA:37:91-92, NJ State Archives Trenton); inventory £472:10:6 taken 1792-06-07 by John Stillwell + Isaac Covenhoven. Estate distribution: son Jacob residuary legatee; daughters Nelly Lane / Sarah Smock / Caty Lane (married daughters); wife Mary support. Executors brother-in-law Garret Hendrickson + friend Jacob Covenhoven.

people.notable_records: 1765-10-08 Will / proved 1766-04-23 (NJA:33:93): Matthias Covenhoven of Middletown NJ named wife Williampe; sons William, Matthias, Jacob, Peter; daughters Sarah, Jane, Williampe, Ann. Executors: Daniel son of John Hendrickson "my sister's son" + Cornelius Covenhoven son of William Covenhoven deceased. Witnesses: Cornelius Luyster, Joseph Golden, Richard Stillwell. Inventory taken 1765-10-25 by Joseph Golden + Richard Crawford = £1,254:5:0 (LARGEST inventory among Jacob Willemse's 9 children — strong enslaver-economy indicator). 1743-07-05 will of father Jacob Willemse bequeathed "my Negroe Woman Betty" — specifically named bequest.

people.children_summary: TWELVE CHILDREN per FS GSRN-X37 family group; 9 of 12 named or counted in 1777 will. SONS (4): (3) Pieter Kouwenhoven (1753-1787, FS LZFS-WFF) — eldest son; named co-executor; co-inherited home farm; died 10 yrs after father at 34. (5) William Kouwenhoven (1758-1825, FS L6MR-8DT) — = MATCH; named executor at 19; co-inherited home farm. (7) Garret Kouwenhoven (1761-1784, FS LCM3-7G7) — bequeathed Stintenberg tract + £100 building money; died at 23 = 7 yrs after father; FS-CONFIRMED death year rules him out as the 1815-1817 Flatlands Town Clerk Gerrit (candidate, who must be a grandson per feedback_flatlands_town_clerk_succession.md). (9) Luke Kouwenhoven (1766-1853, FS KC9H-7LQ) — = MATCH; bequeathed plantans tract + £150 + Patentee Birth Right; lived ~87 yrs. DAUGHTERS (8 total per FS, 5 in 1777 will count): (1) Antje Kouwenhoven (1749-1751, FS LZFS-W8J) — infant death, predeceased father, NOT in will. (2) Anna Kouwenhoven (1751-Deceased, FS KP47-S4V) — = the "5th unnamed daughter" of the will's page-4 "five Daughters" total: counted but NOT named in £50 clause; age 26 in 1777, likely already married + provided for at marriage per Dutch convention. (4) Ida (Eidah) Kouwenhoven (1756-1794, FS LHWL-2MP) — age 21 in 1777; £50 clause as "Eidah." (6) Abigail #1 Covenhoven (1760-Deceased no year, FS MCLD-LHC) — almost certainly died in childhood before 1768; the next-born sister (#10) was given the same name per Dutch reuse-of-name pattern. (8) Seitje Kouwenhoven (1763-1837, FS LHWL-238) — age 14 in 1777; £50 clause as "Seitie." (10) Abigail #2 Kouwenhoven (1768-1795, FS K2YS-DRQ) — age 9 in 1777; £50 clause as "abbigal" (memorial-name for Abigail #1). (11) Jannetje Kouwenhoven (1771-1832, FS K4NG-9H7) — age 6 in 1777; £50 clause as "Iannetie." (12) Catryntje Kouwenhoven (1773-Deceased no year, FS 9NTV-2R4) — age 4 at will date but NOT in £50 clause; ANOMALY. Best explanations: (a) predeceased the will (b.1773 d. before 1777-09-13 = consistent with FS "Deceased" no year); (b) provided for in residuary clause separately; (c) clause omitted her by oversight. (a) most likely and matches the childhood-mortality pattern of 3 daughters lost (Antje #1, Abigail #1, Catryntje). NOTE: The will's page-4 totals "Nine Children" / "five Daughters" reflect the SURVIVING children at will date (4 sons + 5 daughters living = Anna, Ida, Abigail #2, Seitje, Jannetje). Antje #1 + Abigail #1 + Catryntje all already deceased by 1777. The £50 clause names only the 4 youngest unmarried daughters; Anna (already-married) is the silent 5th of the page-4 count.
people.sources: Will of Garret Kouwenhoven (NYSA J0038-92 SS2 2869), dated 1777-09-13 Flatlands, proved 1778-01-15 before Surrogate Cary Ludlow City & County of NY, recorded 1778-03-04 — 6 manuscript pages + cover, photographed by Chris 2026-04-26 and analyzed in
people.notable_records: 1777-09-13 WILL of Garret Kouwenhoven (NYSA J0038-92 SS2 2869), Flatlands, Kings Co. NY: dated 1777-09-13; sworn-in/proved 1778-01-15 before Surrogate CARY LUDLOW, City and County of New York; recorded 1778-03-04. WITNESSES (3, Kings Co. yeomen): Abraham Voorhees, Peter Wyckoff, Iohannes [Johannes] Ditmars (Ditmars also named as land-neighbor in salt-meadows clause). EXECUTORS (4, all sworn 1778-01-15): Nicholas Schanck (Schenck), John Ranson, Peter Kouwenhoven (eldest son), William Kouwenhoven (son, age 19). ENSLAVED PERSONS NAMED IN WILL: TWO unnamed Negro wenches devised to widow Antie's widowhood use. LAND BEQUESTS: home farm "on the South Side" + buildings to Peter + William jointly (equal divide); "Stintenberg" tract + £100 building money to Garret; "plantans" + half "wholepoint" + £150 building money + Patentee Birth Right (Flatlands common-lands) to Luke; "Lyon Hook" East-Side-of-Big-Swamp piece divided ¼ each across 4 sons (Luke gets W end); piece of Salt+fresh meadows + Low Land on South+West side of "frozen back" divided ¼ each; 2 wood lots in Flatlands Kill (40 ac + 10 ac) divided ¼ each; salt meadows in New Utrecht to Luke. WIFE'S PROVISIONS: farm income for child-rearing during widowhood; £100 if she remarries; after youngest comes of age, downsizes to one room + 2 milk cows pasturing with sons' herd + free garden + orchard fruit + £30/yr (1/9 from each child) + 1 bushel Indian corn + 1 bushel rye + 0.5 bushel wheat per child per year + her two Negro wenches. RESIDUE: distributed equally among all 9 children; sons pay £300 each toward real estate; daughters get £240 each.
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Two unnamed Negro wenches devised to widow Antie's widowhood use per 1777 will).
people.will_probate: Will of Garret Kouwenhoven, NYSA J0038-92 SS2 2869, dated 1777-09-13 / proved 1778-01-15 / recorded 1778-03-04, Surrogate Cary Ludlow, City and County of New York.

unnamed_groups.record_type: will (devise of life-tenancy in enslaved persons to surviving spouse during widowhood)
unnamed_groups.event_date: 1777-09-23 to ~1782 (effective period — from cp_ens_027 Gerret's death to widow Antie's death; her widowhood window per FS death year 1782)
unnamed_groups.grouping_language: two Negro wenches
unnamed_groups.source_citation: Will of Garret Kouwenhoven of Flatlands, NYSA J0038-92 SS2 2869, dated 1777-09-13 (proved 1778-01-15 before Surrogate Cary Ludlow, City & County of NY; recorded 1778-03-04). Page 4 clause: "after my youngest Living Child is Come to age or marries from that Time my wife antie Shall have one Room for her to Live in and two milk Cows in Pasture with Either of her Sons Cows and free planting in the Garden and Gitting fruit out of the orchards & Shall have two Negro wenches to Serve her During her Widowhood and from the Same time Shall Receive Every Year thirty pounds from my Nine Children Each of them to pay their Equal Ninth part towards it and Each of them to give her one Bushel of Indian [Indigion] Corn one Bushel of Rie and half a Bushel of wheat Every Year." Plural "wenches" + "Widowhood" duration confirmed via Chris's magnified close-up of page 4, 2026-04-26.

people.children_summary: Per FS LTK3-SJC + FindAGrave 232809291 + Franklin Ellis 1885: 5 children with first wife Neeltje Van Mater — Jacoba Gerrit Covenhoven 1749-1812 (FS L9CX-L3K, MATCHES "Cobauchy" deceased daughter named in 1812 will, "Cobauchy" = phonetic rendering of "Jacoba"); Benjamin Garret Covenhoven 1753-1801 (FS LKH1-6BK, MATCHES will's "deceased son Benjamin" with 7 named grandchildren John, Obadiah, Garret, Catherine, Martha, Elenor, Betsey); Catherine Conover 1755-deceased (FS GBBD-X4R, MATCHES will's "Catharine"); John Conover 1766-1775 (FS 9J4V-5S5, died young — NOT the "John" in will); Garret Covenhoven 1770-1851 (FS L6WR-RFL, MATCHES will's "Garret Kovenhoven Jr." who received Jack). 5 children with second wife Antje Schenck — Eleanor 1787-12-13 (m. De Lafayette Schenck — MATCHES will son-in-law executor "Delafayette Schenck"); Jane 1789-11-09 (m. Stacy Prickot); Ann 1790-09 (m. 1st William Schenck, 2nd Theodore Rue); John 1791-12-17 (m. Ann Smock — MATCHES will's "John" co-heir of homestead receiving Callaman); Peter G. 1797-01-02 (m. Charlotte Lyell 1819 — MATCHES will's "Peter" co-heir of homestead receiving Joe; "G." patronymic = Garret's son).

people.notable_records: 1752-07-29: purchased Thomas, Jude, and an unnamed infant from Daniel Walker (Covenhoven Family Papers, Monmouth County Historical Association). | 1793-09-11: composed will (Freehold Twsp., Monmouth Co.; executors Stophel Logan and Tobias Polhemus; witnesses Garret Covenhoven, Ruth Covenhoven, and Joseph Throckmorton). Specifically devised "negro woman named Jane" to widow Mary for life use; directed residuary enslaved people to be divided among 5 children. | 1802-01-23: died, Freehold Twsp. | 1802-02-16: inventory taken by David Gordon and Garret Covenhoven; $888.50 total personal estate, including "5 negroes" (Elizabeth Samuel Simon Pheabe Rachel). File 8479 M. | 1802-04-15: will proved; Lib. 40, p. 46. | EVENT (Will 1793-09-11). | EVENT (Sale 1752-07-29).

Freehold-Middletown DRC; will 1790-09-15 / proved 1810-07-25 NJA:41:88 names sons Garret + Tunis + daughters Eleena, Yanney/Ariantje, Janitje, Merget/Grietje, Ephamy/Aefje, Atkey/Altje): (a) Garret bap. 1753-09-30 d.y.; (b) Eleena bap. 1755-01-26; (c) Ariantje bap. 1757-04-17, perh. m. Joseph Willett; (d) Grietje bap. 1759-05-09 m. Jacob Allen; (e) Janitje (twin to Grietje) bap. 1759-05-09; (f) Garret bap. 1761-12-25 m. Nellie Heyer d. 1817-06-13; (g) Aefje bap. 1764-04-30 m. John Willett; (h) Altje bap. 1766-05-25; (i) Tunis bap. 1769-08-27 m. Martha Vanderhoef d. 1844-04-11; (j) Elizabeth bap. 1775-01-15 (not in father's will).
people.sources: New Jersey, U.S., Abstract of Wills, 1670-1817 — Volume XXXXI, Abstracts of Wills, 1810-1813. Will of William Covenhoven of Freehold dated 1790-09-15, proved 1810-07-25; File 8846 M. Source URL: https://www.ancestry.com/search/collections/2793/. Cocheu 82:4 #639 - primary identification + spouse + 10-children list. Will 1790-09-15 / proved 1810-07-25 NJA:41:88
people.notable_records: 1790-09-15 will (proved 1810-07-25). Wife Elizabeth gets remainder of moveable estate for life; sons Garret + Tunis inherit all lands in Freehold and pay debts; daughters Eleanor + Atkey get beds + bedding. Executors: sons Garret + Tunis (signed as Conover at probate). Witnesses: Stephen Van Brokel, Aron VanderBilt, John Tise. 1810-08-18 inventory $482.11 made by Derick Zutphen, Matthias Van Brakle — lists carpenter's tools and the beds bequeathed to Eleanor and "Alchey" by their father's will. File 8846 M. NO ENSLAVED PEOPLE in will or inventory abstract.
people.lineage: Wolphert > Gerrit > Jan > Gerret > William > William

people.children_summary: 11 per Cocheu 81:3 #354 + FS MJ33-KMJ: (a) John (b. 1757-04-25, FS L69X-5CT); (b) Elias⁷ (b. 1759-02-04, FS L69X-5Z6; d. 1786-11-15 drowning at Powles-Hook ferry, age 27; merchant at Allentown NJ; left a young family; per). Margaret Anderson 1780); (f) Jane (b. 1767-10-19, m. Conover of Upper Freehold" per (1826 manumitter of Cloe Jeffers); (h) Forman (b. 1773-06-06, FS KH7J-3TM); (i) Mary (b. 1775-07-06); (j) Eleanor (b. 1776-11-26, d. 1852, m. Dr. Joseph Leedom 1804, FS L69X-5FR); (k) William (b. 1778-08-20, FS L69J-4RJ). Note Forman-line maternal-honor naming pattern: 3 of 11 children carry "Forman" as given/middle name (one died young, replaced by another bearing the same name).
people.sources: Cocheu 81:3 #354; Monmouth Surrogate Probate File 8481 M, Lib. 39 p. 519
people.notable_records: (1802-03-23 intestate probate inventory, Monmouth Surrogate File 8481 M, Lib. 39 p. 519; administered under 1799 NJ Act for insolvent estates by Anthony F. Taylor of Burlington Co.). Inventory included 1 Negro Wench Charlotte ($100), 1 ditto Bound to A. Cox until age 18 Elizabeth ($20), and an Article of Agreement with Wm Lloyd referencing an unnamed Negro man whose survival triggered installment payments 1803-1804 ($117.87 owed). | (1782-02-08 taken prisoner by Loyalist refugees under Lt. Stevenson during Pleasant Valley raid; captured with Hendrick Hendrickson + 2 sons, Garret Hendrickson, Samuel Bowne + son, and Jacques Denice; rescued by Capt. John Schenck of Col. Holmes's regiment — Patriot political alignment attested).
people.enslaved_people_linked: Charlotte (1802 probate inventory, appraised $100, "Negro Wench") Elizabeth (1802 probate inventory, appraised $20, "ditto Bound to A. Cox untill the age of 18"); plus an Negro man tied to a Wm Lloyd agreement.
people.will_probate: Intestate. Administration File 8481 M, Lib. 39 p. 519, granted 1802-03-23 to Anthony F. Taylor of Burlington Co. (non-Cowenhoven administrator — implies family did not administer). Insolvent estate under 1799 NJ Act.

people.mother_text: Williampe Wall | 2026-05-12 per FS GJ7Y-PV2: Willemse Wall (FS KCBQ-VR9, 1709-1759)
people.spouse_text: (1) m. 1755-12-08 Eleanor Wyckoff b. 1732 (or 1737) d. 1793-08-26 — dau. of William + Agnes Van Doren Wyckoff (NJA:22:76; NJMis:2:310); (2) m. Mrs. Eleanor (Neeltje) Williamson Fields #351 — b. 1743-09-30 d. ~1830 — dau. of Aeltie + William Williamson, widow of Michael Fields of Bound Brook NJ; she m. (3rd) ~1826 John Bray of New Brunswick NJ | 2026-05-12 FS GJ7Y-PV2 CONFIRMS sequential wives: (1st) Eleanor 'Nelly' Wyckoff (FS L63C-T3P, 1732-1793) m. 1755-12-08 Monmouth NJ; (2nd) — closes the long-standing 'Eleanor second-wife identity' open question from Blog Post Idea #1 + 2026-04-25 research.
people.children_summary: Per 1802 will: son Peter Covenhoven (heir to all lands); son Elias (executor); eldest dau. Nelly [surname unread]; dau. Agnes (her daughters Eleanor & Nelly named as granddaughters); grandson John Covenhoven (gets goods + books). Per FS GJ7Y-PV2: first-marriage children Col Elias Covenhoven (1757-1820, FS KLQS-ZTM, the executor) + Agnes Johnse Covenhoven (1758-1832, FS KLQS-Z4V); second-marriage children list cut off in screenshot — likely contains the 'son Peter' and 'eldest dau. Nelly' of the will. Agnes's married surname unresolved across aggregators (WikiTree-snippet 'Remsen' / FG 'Quay' / FS just 'Covenhoven' maiden) — primary marriage record needed. [2026-05-05 UPDATE] Col Elias 1757-1820 CONFIRMED; his son ('s grandson) Elias jr 1790-1826 CONFIRMED.
people.sources: PRIMARY: 1802-12-14 will + 1803 probate, FamilySearch DGS 007637520 frames 00719-00729 (= Monmouth Probate File 5980 M)
people.notable_records: Covenhoven Justice' officiated Garret Covenhoven Jr. × Ruth Stout 1796-01-31 (cert dated 1796-02-29, recorded 1796-03-08) + Barance Hagerman × Mary Pettinger 1795-09-19 (same recording day). stature (Continental Army Colonel, DAR Patriot, Freehold proprietor) consistent with JP appointment. ALTERNATIVE candidate: John P. Covenhoven (1740-1811, also Freehold Monmouth). Resolution needs NJ JP commission records 1790-1803. | (1779-04-26 captured by British during Shrewsbury raid, along with his son Elias, age 22 at capture; carried off to NY by British detachment of 700 men; "justice Covenhoven and son" per Penna Evening Post Apr 30 1779).
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Mock) | (Diana) | (Jennet) | (Phillip) | (Rachel) | (Samuel) | (Dinah) | (Cezar) | (Suki) | (Rose) — all MATCH per 1802-12-14 will + 1803 probate inventory Agnes Forman | Sally Forman | Elen Forman | Anna Forman | Molly Forman — daughters of Ezekiel Forman, subjects of 1795 Writ of Habeas Corpus (Case 34542) attributed to per 2026-05-08 Phase 3a disambiguation. The Esq. honorific in the case file reflects this John's NJ legislative role (NJ Assembly 1776 + Constitution drafter) and Stockton-prisoner standing. None of the 5 Forman daughters appears in his 1802 will, suggesting the 1795 HC case freed them.
people.will_probate: Monmouth Probate File 5980 M; will dated 1802-12-14; witnesses Lewis Gordon, David Hance, John Vanderveer' in 2nd slot which is now confirmed as David Hance.
people.burial_location: Old Brick Reformed Church Cemetery, Marlboro, Monmouth Co. NJ (FG Memorial #133000155, family panel CONFIRMED 2026-05-20)

people.sources: Stiles, A History of the City of Brooklyn, vol. 1 (1867): p. 244 [1775 Kings Co. committee delegate to the Provincial Convention]; p. 251 fn 1 [co-owner of 'Cowenhoven's boschje'/Fort Putnam hill, 'his son, Rem Cowenhoven']; pp. 316-317 [1780 loyalist address to Gov. Robertson, signer]; Map of Bedford Corners (btwn pp. 266-267) large fig. #21 [Rem Cowenhoven, Bedford farm; attribution tentative]. Transcribed in project Bibliography (Stiles entries) 2026-06-08
people.notable_records: 1777-02-10 placed runaway advertisement offering reward for return of Jeffery Johnson 'Jaff'; signed oath of loyalty to British Crown December 1776 (Loyalist); 1775-06-01 bill of sale for canoe with Hendrick Brant of New York City. | 1782-01-12 Last Will and Testament executed — signed with his mark X 'with the assistance of Nicholas Cowenhoven one of the deponants' (= his brother Judge Nicholas), being 'very sick in body but of sound disposing mind.' Three executors named: brother Nicholas, son John, and Peter Lefferts. Will proved 1783-02-10 before Cary Ludlow Esq., Surrogate for City and Province of New York; letters issued 1 March 1783 under prerogative seal. PARCEL DEVISES: clause 3 — homestead farm 90 acres → John; clause 4 — Derick-in-Camps ~23 ac → John; clause 5 — part of the Hoek ~21 ac → John; clause 6 — Yellow Waters Camp / Geele Waters Kamp 27 ac → John; clause 7 — Roycraft farm (rented to Mr. Ryerson) → Nicholas; clause 8 — Aughter-Camp / Aughtieste Kamp 27 ac → Nicholas; clause 9 — Wallabocht meadow + 2 ac → Nicholas; clause 10 — share of mother's homestead's Brooklyn Homestead → executors empowered to sell for debts/legacies; clause 11 — undivided residue → John + Nicholas as tenants in common; clause 12 — £1000 legacy → daughter Caty; clause 13 — £700 legacy → daughter Sally; clause 14 — household goods + livestock + farming utensils + 'slaves' → all four children equally divided. | 1783-02-10 Probate Inventory taken — total estate £4,977: bonds + notes + cash £3,565-13-4 + goods + chattels £1,412-2-0 (incl. 8 enslaved at total £275). Inventory appraised by John Cowenhoven (likely brother — see follow_ups; conflict-of-interest with son as estate-buyer) + Leffert Lefferts Esq. CLOSING TRANSITION: son John R. took entire personal estate at appraisement, becoming new enslaver of all 8 named individuals. | 1778-05-25 NY Gazette & Weekly Mercury sale ad offering for cash an unnamed ~28-yr-old enslaved woman (skilled domestic, smallpox survivor, lived chiefly in NYC) and her ~7-mo-old female child — placed just 24 days after Rem's father's death (1778-05-01) and 7 days before the June 1, 1778 probate inventory.
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Sam) (Oldest Harry) (Young Jack) (Old Jack) (Tom adult) (small-boy Harry) (Sare) (Sare's child Tom) (unnamed woman + her ~7-mo-old daughter; 1778-05-25 sale ad)
people.will_probate: Will dated 1782-01-14 / proved 1783-02-10 (WNYHS:12:34-36): names sons John + Nicholas + daughters Catharine + Sarah; references "the homestead where my mother now lives." Executors: brother Nicholas + son John + Peter Lefferts.
people.lineage: Wolphert > Gerrit > Jan > Nicasius > John > Rem

people.mother_text: Antje Dorlandt Van Pelt (1712-unknown) — per Find a Grave family panel; primary support pending
people.spouse_text: Nicasius (Nicholas) Cowenhoven — Gerret's deceased son per 1781 will; gravestone reads 'wife of Nicholas Cowenhoven' (Nicholas = Anglicized Nicasius). MARRIAGE DATE: WikiTree Van_Pelt-274 lead asserts 1778-10-18 in Brooklyn, but this conflicts with son Garrett's primary-confirmed birth 1774-05-02 — marriage would post-date Garrett's birth by 4.5 years. Either (a) WikiTree date wrong, (b) earlier marriage existed, or (c) 1778-10 might actually be Nicasius's death. Required documentation.
people.children_summary: Per Gerret's 1781 will: Nicasius and Nelly's children = '6 children: granddaughters Antie, Geertie, Sarah, Elizabeth, Nieltie + grandson Garret' — 5 daughters + 1 son. Son: Garrett (1774-1840).
people.sources: GRAVESTONE — New Utrecht Cemetery, Bensonhurst, Kings Co. NY: 'In memory of NELLY VAN PELT, wife of Nicholas Cowenhoven, who departed this life Sep[r] 21st 1817, aged 82 years 2 months 4 days.'; the marriage date conflicts with Garrett's 1774 birth and is suspect
people.notable_records: (1) GRAVESTONE — New Utrecht Cemetery, Bensonhurst, Kings Co. NY: 'In memory of NELLY VAN PELT, wife of Nicholas Cowenhoven, who departed this life Sep[r] 21st 1817, aged 82 years 2 months 4 days.' Find a Grave Memorial #66234788 (https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/66234788) — multiple gravestone photographs (overview by S Sh, detail by Jackie). (2) GERRET'S 1781 WILL — names 'widow Nieltie' (= Nelly) + 6 children of deceased son Necasie; primary kin anchor. NO Active-sheet linkages currently attributed to her — pre-dates 1799 NY law birth-indenture records that document Garrett 1803-1817; pre-emancipation-era manumission/sale records may surface under variant Anglicized name (Eleanor / Helen / Widow / Mrs.).
people.burial_location: New Utrecht Cemetery, Bensonhurst, Kings County, New York. Find a Grave Memorial #66234788 (https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/66234788) — multiple gravestone photographs (overview by S Sh, detail by Jackie).

people.spouse_text: Catharine Suydam (b. ~1737, d. 1815-04-22 in her 78th year per Cocheu 82:4 #661; daughter of Cornelius Suydam per his 1768-12-12 will NJA:34:513 mentioning "daughter Catharine, wife of Nicholas Covenhoven"). m. ~1758. Both Nicholas and Catharine bur.
people.children_summary: Per 1802 will (manuscript): son CORNELIUS COVENHOVEN (co-executor; inherits SW half of 280-acre plantation including ~140 ac + half salt meadow; pays £225 York money to daughters/granddaughters; £150 bond he owed testator was forgiven; receives Jack immediately at testator's death — candidate). Son NICHOLAS COVENHOVEN. Daughter CATHARINE COVENHOVEN (£75 from Cornelius + £75 from Nicholas = £150 York money in two installments at 2 + 4 yrs from testator's decease + share of widow's wearing apparel at widow's death). Daughter ELIZABETH COVENHOVEN (£75 from Cornelius + £75 from Nicholas = £150 York money in two installments at 3 + 5 yrs from testator's decease + share of widow's wearing apparel at widow's death). Deceased son PETER COVENHOVEN (predeceased; survived by daughters). Granddaughter CATHARINE COVENHOVEN (Peter's daughter; share of £75+£75=£150 York money at 6+7 yrs from testator's decease, payable when she comes of age). Granddaughter ELIZABETH COVENHOVEN (Peter's daughter; share of £150 York money at same schedule as sister). Total bequest to daughters + granddaughters = £450 York money paid in installments over 7 yrs. | FamilySearch IDs: son Cornelius Covenhoven; son Nicholas Covenhoven; deceased son Peter (1766-1802, FS KHLF-8BN); daughter Catharine (1759-1827, FS 2CB5-6PS); daughter Elizabeth (1761-1855, FS K8BP-RZB); widow Catryna Suydam (1738-1815, FS K693-M5Z) ← the previously-unnamed widow of the 1802 will; outlived testator by 12 years.
people.sources: PRIMARY: 1802-10-19 will of Nicholas Covenhoven of Franklin Twsp Somerset Co NJ; 1803-05-30 inventory; 1803-06-22 probate. Manuscript pages:
people.notable_records: 1802-10-19 will composed at Franklin Twsp Somerset Co NJ (manuscript signed "Nicholas Covenhoven {LS}"); 1803-05-30 inventory taken (£556 3s 0d; appraisers Richard Fulkerson + Farrington Berkelue [Fereton Barkalow]); 1803-06-22 will proved before Surrogate John Neilson Esq. (Somerset Co.); probate granted by Hon. John Lambert Esq. (NJ Vice President of the Council); recorded by John Beatty[?]; will book Lib. 40 pp. 339-340 (manuscript pagination); File 1219 R; primary manuscript pages at | FG Memorial #19078165 for son Nicholas gravestone PRIMARY: 'In memory of Nicholas Covenhoven, who departed this life March 8, 1807, aged 34 years 2 months 8 days' — back-calc birth ~1772-12-31; burial Covenhoven Family Burying Ground, Franklin Twsp Somerset Co NJ; spouse Mrs Hannah Toost Covenhoven d.1804; child Peter 1793-1794 (infant).
people.enslaved_people_linked: (Jack — devised IMMEDIATELY to son Cornelius at testator's death; possibly = the Jack age 12 at $80 in 1811 inventory if Cornelius transferred Jack to brother Nicholas before 1811) | (Frank — devised to son Nicholas DEFERRED until widow's death; possibly = the Frank at $110 in 1811 inventory if widow died 1803-1811). Linkage lives on the Active rows' additional_events column AI remains discovery anchor for all four, and Izabel is the one single-event person of the four (no 1811-inventory reappearance). | NOTE 2026-04-25: Jack's primary AG anchor moved → (Cornelius) this pass still appears at this discovery anchor because the testator devised him to Cornelius IMMEDIATELY at father's death (testator routes Jack to son even though son becomes the holding enslaver). Discovery-anchor enslaver; testator/routing-enslaver.

people.notable_records: Will dated 1801-09-14 / proved 1803-03-21 (NJA:39:101-02): William Covenhoven of South Brunswick, Middlesex Co. NJ; mentioned wife Elizabeth; daughters Elizabeth Wyckoff, Margaret Schenck, Anne Bergen, Mary Covenhoven; sons Luke, David, Elias, Johnson, William. Witnesses: David Chambers, William Jordan, Cornelius Hulick. Note: 5/6 session memo records "4 enslaved already linked" to William W. Sr. — those linkages need cross-sheet audit (no cp_ens_HELD placeholder rows found 2026-05-10).
people.enslaved_people_linked: WILL ABSTRACT REVIEWED 2026-05-29 (Ancestry NJ Abstract of Wills 1670-1817, Vol XXXIX p.101-102): no enslaved persons named in any bequest. INVENTORY total $3,944.98 (File 9960 L) — large enough to potentially contain enslaved persons (comparable to's $4,232.94 1815 inventory which DID name 2 enslaved persons), but abstract shows only the total without itemization. Abstract reliability check: contemporary 1793 abstract for Cornelius A. Covenhoven (same Volume series) EXPLICITLY names 'negro woman named Jane' in bequests — confirms Ancestry abstractors DO surface enslaved-person mentions when present in source. The silence here is therefore meaningful but not conclusive (inventory itemization could still surface persons abstract missed). INHERITANCE CHAIN: sons Johnson (7 enslaved persons 1811-1822) + William Jr. (4 enslaved persons 1814) JOINTLY INHERITED the Cranbury Neck plantation in 1803. Enslaved mothers in their post-1803 records (Kate, Betty, Nancy, Hannah) were plausibly children/teens in's household at his 1803 death — but plausibility is not documentation. Surrogate's records / NJ State Archives) — would settle the question; (b) NJ Tax Ratables for South Brunswick Twp ~1790s-1800 if surviving — slave column; (c) Design decision on demote-without-PID-rename workflow per session-log 2026-05-29. NOTE: NJ federal census schedules for 1800/1810/1820 are LOST (per session-log) — that path is not available.
people.will_probate: Will dated 1801-09-14 / proved 1803-03-21 — NJA Vol XXXIX (Calendar of Wills 1801-1805) p.101-102; original Liber 40 p.379 (Middlesex Co.). Will-text abstract reviewed via Ancestry NJ Abstract of Wills 2026-05-29; bequests: wife Elizabeth bed/bedding + £28 yearly in lieu of dower; daughters Elizabeth Wicoff/Wyckoff £100, Marget Schenck £100, Anne Bergen £100, Mary £100; son Lukes £375 (including obligations); son David £100 (including note); son Elias house+lot in Cranbury (bought of Benjamin Armstrong); sons Johnson + William JOINT inheritance of 'part of plantation south of the road across from Cranbury Neck' + 40 acres woodland adjoining Daniel Duncan, paying £8 yearly during mother's life; rest divided among 9 surviving children. Executors: son Lukes + sons-in-law John Wicoff + Joseph Schenck + John P. Bergin. Witnesses: David Chambers, William Jordon, Cornelius Hulick. 1803-05-18 Inventory $3,944.98 by David Chambers + Samuel Karr [Kerr], File 9960 L.

people.will_probate: 1777-10-07 (executed) / 1778-05-30 (proved) — NJA Vol XXXIV (Calendar of Wills 1771-1780) p.292; original Liber 20 p.196 (Middlesex Co.). Will-text abstract reviewed via Ancestry NJ Abstract of Wills 2026-05-29; bequests: wife Deborah all moveable estate; sons William + Ruliff Covenhoven the mansion house + plantation + all real estate (they to pay £200 to 3 sisters Margret, Deborah, Mary); wife to have use of plantation until son William turns 21. Executors: Garret Schenck + John Schenck (son of Ruliff), both of Penn's Neck. Witnesses: Samuel Oldden, Thomas + Joseph Skelton. 1778-05-29 Inventory £571.5.0 made by John Schenck Jr. + Thomas Skelton. [Cocheu NYG&BR 82:4 #574 — Cocheu cited "NJA:34:291" but actual page is 292; off-by-one corrected 2026-05-29].

people.notable_records: (a) Beneficiary of John Covenhoven's 1802-12-14 will — receives "all the negroes that came by her excepting the issue thereof"; (b) Inventoried as widow-recipient of (Cezar) (Suki), and (Rose) in the 1803 probate sub-block "to the widow by will" (FamilySearch DGS 007637520 frame 00727); (c) 1812-06-03 manumission of Ceazer (formerly) — manumitter recorded as "Eleanor Covenhoven"; (d) 1815-02-25 + 1816-08-28 BBB registrations of James Henry and Ephraim sons of Rachel, filed as "Eleanor Conover of the Village & Township of Freehold"; (e) 1822-08-26 manumission of Rachel age ~24 (Monmouth Manumission Book pp. 88-89, full statutory wrapper; recorded 1822-09-06; consolidated 2026-05-05 with the BBB mother row). (f) Own 1817 Will — testamentary manumission clause for 5 enslaved persons (Phillis, Jacob, James, Leah, Rachel); probated 1830-06-05 by John Bray (3rd husband, co-executor) + Garret Wyckoff (nephew, son of sister Patience Williamson Wyckoff).

people.children_summary: George Cowenhoven (b.1764-06-09, d.1805-04-13 aged 40); John Nicholas "John N." Cowenhoven (1769-1806, documented inheritor of New Utrecht properties at father's 1793 death per existing row notes — planned ENSLAVER row Stage C-3); other children pending Chris's primary research. 1790 federal census Town of New Utrecht confirms Judge Nicholas + son George as first two heads of household in the township; the 1790 household tally [4 white M 16+; 0 white M <16; 2 white F; 1 OFP; 10 SLAVES; total 17] suggests at least 4 adult males in the household — Judge Nicholas + John N. (age 21) + 2 unidentified — likely additional adult sons or extended family pending primary research.
people.sources: Stiles, A History of the City of Brooklyn, vol. 1 (1867): pp. 242-243 [Lieut.-Col., Kings Co. militia, 1776]; pp. 296-297 [defection/British commissary dealings/treason indictment escaped via A. Hamilton/Chief Judge of Common Pleas/d. New Utrecht 6 Mar 1793]; pp. 316-317 [1780 loyalist address, 'M.' (recte N.) Cowenhoven signer]. Transcribed in project Bibliography (Stiles entries) 2026-06-08 | Cocheu, The Van Kouwenhoven-Conover Family (NYG&BR; 1989 ed.), #654, p. 84 [Judge Nicholas, son of John Senior #214; b.1744 d.1793; first Judge of the Inferior Court of Common Pleas, Kings Co.; m. Jannetje Lott]
people.notable_records: Account and receipt books 1782-1787 at Center for Brooklyn History (Cowenhoven receipt books, NYU finding aid bcms_0095) — include receipts for purchases of enslaved people from neighbors and cousins. Cowenhoven-Benson mansion in Town of New Utrecht (present-day Bath Beach, Brooklyn) — known for a millstone doorstep, basement chains used to punish the enslaved, and entertaining George Washington and other luminaries. Buried at New Utrecht Reformed Church cemetery alongside wife Jannetje (Jane) Lott. 1790 FEDERAL CENSUS — Town of New Utrecht, Kings County NY (Household #3, first head of household in New Utrecht). The 'all other free persons: 1' = a free Black household member (free Black labor co-resident with enslaved labor — unusual to document); the 4 free white males 16+ include Judge Nicholas himself + adult son John N. (age 21) + 2 unidentified adult males. Adjacent Household #3a is son George Cowenhoven — also independently enslaving 4 people. Combined Cowenhoven New Utrecht enslaved-person count in 1790 = 14 across the two households. | (1792-03-01 chaired Kings County Freeholders' meeting at Dr. James Van Beuren's house, Flatbush, signed as "NICHOLAS COWENHOVEN, Chairman" per Daily Advertiser Mar 3, 1792; unanimously endorsed Gov. George Clinton for re-election and Pierre Van Cortlandt for Lt. Gov.; meeting also appointed a 28-member Committee of Correspondence including himself and his brother to promote Clinton's candidacy). Confirms his active civic leadership 1 year + 6 days before death (1793-03-07).
people.burial_location: New Utrecht Cemetery, Bensonhurst, Kings County, New York. Find a Grave Memorial #66234789 (https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/66234789/nicholas-cowenhoven) — gravestone photograph by Christopher Barnes (Chris).

people.children_summary: Per Cocheu 82:4 #659 (p. 224) — TEN children across THREE marriages: BY 1st WIFE Margaret Ammerman (m. 1768-02-28, d. 1780-09-22): (a) Antje⁷ b. 1769-07-23, d. 1845-08-16, m. Jacobus Van Nuys; (b) Jannetje⁷ b. 1771-09-17, d. 1774-09-06 (d.y.); (c) Sarah⁷ b. 1774-04-22, d. 1854-04-21, m. 1792-01-14 John Hanse of New Utrecht (at Flatlands). BY 2nd WIFE Elizabeth Van Pelt (m. 1781-08-03, d. 1786-03-23): (d) Gertrude⁷ b. 1782-06-01, d. 1865-04-01, m. 1801-01-08 Roelof Van Brunt; (e) Margaret⁷ b. 1784-05-05, d. 1867-08-01, m. Coert Gerritsen of Somerville NJ; (f) Elizabeth⁷ b. 1786-03-23, d. 1871-02-15, unmar. BY 3rd WIFE Catharine Stellenwerf (m. 1787-06-24): (g) Phebe⁷ b. 1789-03-17, d. 1868-11-01, unmar.; (h) Garret⁷ b. 1791-08-27, d. 1828-09-06, m. 1818-03-11 Catharine Bergen (b. 1791-10-29, d. 1828-10-02 — both died within a month of each other). The 'Garret J.' rendering was confirmed by Chris on 2026-04-25 to be a scribal variant for (Garrett Cowenhoven b. 1774-05-02, d. 1840-01-28, son of Nicasius Cowenhoven by Gerret's branch — i.e., this Garret⁷'s first-cousin, NOT his identical twin). Garret⁷ is therefore NOT the 1822 Nan manumitter; that is. + record + disambiguation note.] (i) Jacob⁷ b. 1795-01-11, d. 1826-07-22. (j) Nicholas⁷ b. 1796-10-30, d. 1841-05-29, m. Matilda Dingee (b. 1807-10-10, d. 1861-01-19). Cocheu cites 'Bergen Fam. 1876, p.329' at end of #659.
people.sources: Children. of New Utrecht; see also)
people.notable_records: (1) 1781-05-09 will of father Gerret (NY Wills Vol 0035-0039 pp.222-225) — Johannes inherits 1/3 of "All my Negroes" + furniture. (2) Town of New Utrecht Births and Manumissions of Slaves: birth indentures under 1799 law for Sare 1806, Mary 1808, Cate 1810, Tom 1813; manumission of Jack age 30 on 1822-05-20 (image #129). (3) Nan manumission 1822-06-06 by Garret J. Cowenhoven names Johannes among "Other people mentioned" — corroborating his presence in New Utrecht as of 1822. (4) 1776-11-13 (publ. 1776-12-09) — subscriber on the Kings County Loyalty Address to Lord Howe and Governor William Tryon, printed in The New-York Gazette and the Weekly Mercury No. 1311 (Hugh Gaine, NY) on the address subscriber list at p.2 middle column. Name printed verbatim as 'James Cowenhoven'; identified as Johannes by Chris 2026-05-17 on the basis of the New Utrecht qualifier and the absence of any other adult Johannes/James/Jacobus Cowenhoven in New Utrecht in 1776. Co-signers from the immediate Cowenhoven kin network: first cousin Rem (Brooklyn), cousin-once-removed Judge Nicholas (New Utrecht), and the two Flatlands Kouwenhoven men (William) and (Gerret Willemse). The 'James'-for-'Johannes' typesetting choice in this print is unusual — Hugh Gaine's shop set four other Dutch 'Johannes' names correctly on the same p.2 column — and is flagged for future investigation. See SOURCE for full context, verbatim transcription, and typesetting note.

people.name: Catherine Stellerwerf
people.name_variations: Catharine Stellerwerf (maiden); Catharine Cowenhoven; Catharine Conover; Mrs. Conover (1830 census rendering)
people.residence_primary: Town of New Utrecht, Kings County, New York
people.occupation: Widow + household manager / enslaver post-1823 (succeeded husband Johannes upon his 1823-09-13 death; documented as "Mrs. Conover" head of household on 1830 federal census, p394, Town of New Utrecht)
people.spouse_text: Johannes Van Couwenhoven, b. 1746-10-04, d. 1823-09-13. Exact-year birth peers (both 1746). Catherine outlived him by ~20 years and ran the New Utrecht household as widow 1823–1843.
people.children_summary: and John N. The Garret J.
people.sources: 1830 FEDERAL CENSUS — Town of New Utrecht, Kings County, NY, page 394 — "Mrs. Conover" entry. FamilySearch image 775-776 of 814, image group 005155934 for image 775; for image 776). NB: FamilySearch indexes this group under "Westchester, New York" but the page margin clearly reads "New Utrecht / Kings County". Local archived copies in
people.notable_records: (1) 1830 FEDERAL CENSUS — Town of New Utrecht, Kings County, NY, page 394, listed as "Mrs. Conover" head of household, 13 persons total. White-side composition (validated against FHNU slide 45 + high-res PDF 1 read 2026-04-25): WHITE MALES — 5-9: 1. WHITE FEMALES — 10-14: 1; 40-49: 2; 70-79: 1 (Catherine herself; her actual age 84 placed her in 80-89 — census-taker under-aged her). Free-colored-side composition (high-res PDF 2 column-placement read 2026-04-25): SLAVES — 0 in all columns; FREE COLORED MALES — Under 10: 2; 10-24: 2; 36-55: 2. FREE COLORED FEMALES — Under 10: 1; 36-55: 1. Total free colored in household = 8; total enslaved = 0. By 1830 Catherine no longer held legally enslaved people, consistent with 1827 NY abolition; the 8 free colored persons are very likely formerly enslaved by Catherine + Johannes pre-1827 and remained as household members post-emancipation. Source: FamilySearch image group 005155934, images 775 + 776 of 814 (record indexed under "Westchester, NY" at FamilySearch but the image margin clearly reads "New Utrecht / Kings County"). (2) Inferred succession to Johannes's New Utrecht enslaver role 1823–1843.
people.enslaved_people_linked: (none on 1830 census — Catherine held 0 enslaved per high-res PDF 2 read; Stage C-4 still needs to scan Active and UNNAMED sheets for pre-1827 enslaver-side rows attributable to "Catharine Cowenhoven" / "Mrs. Conover" / "Mrs. Cowenhoven" / variant Anglicized renderings; Catherine's enslaver role pre-1827 is implied by the 8 free colored persons in her 1830 household)

people.notable_records: 1778-06-01 Probate Inventory of father — drew £723 inheritance portion 'by Consent of his Mother' as one of three executor sons; 30 June 1789 Brooklyn deed — grantee from nephew Nicholas R. of 25-acre Aughtieste Kamp (parcel surrounded by his own land east/south/north + Geele Waters Kamp of John R. Couwenhoven westerly); 1790 US Federal Census Town of Brooklyn — household head with 9 white + 7 enslaved.; 27 Feb – 22 Mar 1794 Daily Advertiser (New York) runaway notice for Anthony, age ~26, self-emancipated from Brooklyn ca. 19 Feb 1794 — reward escalated $10→$30 between 3 and 6 March; outcome unknown. | (1792-03-01 member of Kings County Freeholders' Committee of Correspondence appointed at the Flatbush meeting chaired by his brother Judge Nicholas Cowenhoven; charged with promoting Gov. George Clinton's re-election among NY State citizens; per Daily Advertiser Mar 3, 1792). First documented civic/political role for; brothers jointly mobilizing the Brooklyn-Dutch vote for Clinton in the famously contested 1792 gubernatorial election.

people.children_summary: NOTE: 1790 census shows only 1 wm<16 in his household but FS lists ≥4 sons born by 1790 — discrepancy worth investigating (possible early child deaths not reflected in FS, sons boarded out, or census enumeration error)
people.notable_records: 1777-09-13 Will of father Garret Kouwenhoven (NYSA J0038-92 SS2 2869): named executor at age 19, alongside elder brother Peter, plus non-family executors Nicholas Schanck (Schenck) and John Ranson; co-inherited home farm "on the South Side" with brother Peter, equal division. 1778-01-15 Sworn in as executor before Surrogate Cary Ludlow, City and County of New York (per will probate endorsement page 6). 1778-12-12 Marriage to Annatie Wyckoff at Flatlands. 1790 US Census, Town of Flatlands, Kings County NY (FS ark XHKP-BKX): household 1 wm≥16 / 1 wm<16 / 4 wf / 0 other free / 8 enslaved. 1800 US Census, Flatlands. 1810 US Census, Flatlands. Possibly 1820 "Wm Covenhwen" Flatbush (FS ark XHGZ-X28) but town change introduces ambiguity. Find a Grave Memorial #240115836. Town of Flatlands birth-indenture register entries naming William Kouwenhoven as enslaver (NYC Municipal Archives, Brooklyn Old Town Records, Flatlands; SOURCE-BOTR-Flatlands-Birth_Registers_and_Manumissions_of_Enslaved_People; https://nycrecords.access.preservica.com/uncategorized/IO_c48e45ec-2ae4-43a5-99dd-71a0e3fdb085/): John (b.1800-01-09) Saar (b.1802-09-15, abandonment) Hanna (mother of Saar) Jack (b.1806-10-15) John (b.1807) Dick (b.1808) Isabel (mother of Dick) — 7 rows total = 5 indentured children under 1799 NY Act + 2 enslaved mothers (Hanna, Isabel). | 1792-08-25 JOINT SALE of Bet to nephew Judge Nicholas Covenhoven with co-seller Johannis Remsen for £25 — receipt at Center for Brooklyn History (Nicholas Covenhoven Papers, NYU finding aid bcms_0095) /. Signed 'W[m.] Kouwenhoven' (4-letter Kouw, Flatlands K-spelling). FIRST documented K-spelling Flatlands William attestation in this dataset, predating his 1800-1808 Flatlands BOTR enslaver records by 8 years. Intra-kinship transaction (William=paternal kin via Flatlands branch; Remsen=Nicholas's maternal-line kin via Catharina Remsen) suggests within-family asset reallocation rather than market acquisition.
people.enslaved_people_linked: John Saar Hanna (mother of Saar) Jack John Dick Isabel (mother of Dick). Total now 8 enslaved persons linked to across 1792-1808 window.

people.will_probate: Intestate. Letters of Administration, Monmouth Surrogate File 8480 M, sworn at Freehold 1802-05-29 before Caleb Lloyd, Monmouth County Surrogate; granted to widow Jane (Schenck) Covenhoven and eldest son Elias I. Conover/Covenhoven (b. 1779, age 23 at LOA). $9,812 administration bond same date, payable to Joseph Bloomfield, Esq., Ordinary/Surrogate-General of NJ (then Governor 1801–1812); sureties Daniel Covenhoven (Daniel G., brother per Cocheu #509) and Lewis Gordon (unidentified). Decedent named "John G. Covenhoven" on LOA oath sheet but "John Covenhoven" (without middle initial) in bond text — consistent with "G." as a father-patronymic used selectively rather than as a fixed middle name. Verbatim LOA + bond text on.Source_Quotation.

Echoes the Couwenhoven brothers ↔ Schenck sisters double-marriage in +.
people.children_summary: Per Cocheu 82:4 #617 (p. 217) — FOUR children by his only wife Ann Wyckoff (m. 1790-03-27 Flatlands; b. 1773-06-24 Flatlands, d. 1870-02-20 aged 96, daughter of Joost & Sarah (Luyster) Wyckoff): (a) Antje⁷ b. 1791-03-15, d. 1880-10-23, unmar. (b) George⁷ b. 1792-12-11, d. 1874-11-21 (aged 81 yrs 11 mos 10 dys); m. 1817-12-25 Helena Duryea. (c) Gerrit⁷ b. 1795-05-15, d.y. (infant death). The name was reused for child (d) per Dutch reuse-of-name convention. (d) Gerrit⁷ b. 1797-09-23, d. 1887-01-05, unmar. = (Gerrit Kouwenhoven of Flatlands; 1827 JP candidate + Flatlands inspector of elections per; long-lived bachelor to age 89; after pre-mint scan via Cocheu 82:4 deep-read traced him from the 1827 election notice). Cocheu cites 'Wyckoff Fam. 1934, p.241; Fam. Bible rec.' at end of #617.

people.name: Garret Covenhoven
people.name_variations: Garit Covenhoven (manuscript variant on cert + deed body 1817); Garrit Covenhoven (manuscript variant on deed body 1817); Garret Cowenhoven; Garret Couwenhoven; Garret Conover (orthographic variants per Monmouth Dutch convention; Conover/Covenhoven/Cowenhoven/Couwenhoven all interchangeable for this Dutch lineage). FamilySearch profile ID: L6WR-RFL (matched 2026-05-05 via Chris's screenshot review).
people.residence_primary: Township of Upper Freehold, Monmouth County, New Jersey (per 1817 + 1826 manuscript). Note on Freehold/Upper Freehold ambiguity: his FS-recorded 1792 marriage at 'Freehold, Freehold Township' predates the formal 1798 incorporation of Upper Freehold (NJ Township Act); Upper Freehold had been a distinct geographic area since ~1731 but was administratively merged with Freehold during his early life. The 1792 marriage location is therefore consistent with continuous residence in territory later formalized as Upper Freehold; no relocation required to reconcile the two records.
people.occupation: Yeoman / farmer (presumed for Monmouth Dutch landholder of his generation; FS Sources tab may confirm via census, will, or deed).
people.father_text: Garret Janse Covenhoven (b. 1726-04-27, d. 1812-11-01; FamilySearch ID LTK3-SJC). 'Janse' = Jan-son patronymic, consistent with Monmouth Dutch convention. NOT YET in project FAMILY sheet — cp_fam mint candidate.
people.mother_text: Neeltje Van Mater (b. 1730, d. 1785; FamilySearch ID LY5J-2HK). NOT YET in project FAMILY sheet — cp_fam mint candidate.
people.spouse_text: Maria Gerritse Schenck (b. 1773, d. 1849; FamilySearch ID L6SS-4B2). Married 12 January 1792 at Freehold, Freehold Township, Monmouth County, NJ (per FS — see note in residence_primary on Freehold/Upper Freehold administrative history). Schenck = recurring Monmouth Dutch family in project (cf. R63 Jim Schanck; top of page 116 'Chrineyonce Schenck' deed).
people.children_summary: 9 children with Maria Gerritse Schenck (per FS L6WR-RFL): Eleanor Nelly Couwenhoven (b. 1793, d. 1878; FS L6TP-FTB); William Schenck Conover (b. 1795, d. 1835; FS L6TP-66V) — middle name = mother's maiden name (convention); John Conover (b. 1798, d. 1798 infant; FS K2VL-SHT); Sally Couwenhoven (b. 1799, d. unknown; FS KHKC-HBL); Maria Polhemus Covenhoven (b. 1808, d. 1901; FS K2VL-S6N) — middle name = Polhemus (Dutch family); Catherine Covenhoven (b. 1811, d. 1902; FS L6TL-4XK); Jane Smock Conover (b. 1813, d. 1897; FS LV8J-X7G) — middle name = Smock (Dutch family); John Polhemus Conover (b. 1815, d. 1863; FS KP7P-DFP) — middle name = Polhemus (mother's lineage); Margaret Conover (b. 1819, d. 1854; FS LZ2K-2ZK). PROJECT NOTE: the maternal-surname-as-middle-name pattern that Chris identified for candidate Garret Rezeau Conover is directly attested across FOUR of THIS Garret's children (William Schenck, Maria Polhemus, Jane Smock, John Polhemus) — confirming the convention as widespread across both Garret cousins/lineages in Monmouth Dutch families.
people.sources: PRIMARY: Slave Manumissions, 1787-1844, Monmouth County, NJ — Manumission Book pp.[1817 cert page #unknown] + 169
people.notable_records: 1817-03-10 manumission of George, age ~35, in Township of Upper Freehold; deed signed before Overseers Gilbert Hendrickson + Wm. I. Emley and Justices Wm. I. Emley + Gilbert Hendrickson; recorded by County Clerk C. Lloyd 1817-04-02. Manumission Book p.[unknown]. PARTIAL. 1826-03-13 manumission of Nead, age ~30, in Township of Upper Freehold; deed witnessed by Peter H. Wikoff; acknowledged by Justice of Peace Gilbert Hendrickson. Manumission Book p.169. PARTIAL (cert block + recording stamp on subsequent page).
people.enslaved_people_linked: George (manumitted 1817) Nead (manumitted 1826) Clara — subject of 1813 Writ of Habeas Corpus from NJ Supreme Court (Case 34539); enslaver named in case as 'Garret Covenhoven of Monmouth County' (no township, no middle initial). Three lines tilt to this Garret: (1) no-middle-initial naming matches's 1817+1826 deeds; (2) age/life-stage fit (43 in 1813, just inherited from's Nov 1812 will); (3) multi-decade enslavement pattern matches. SSV00002 Minutes Book 113 pp. 9/62/111/145 review remains path to MATCH. Fanny — TENTATIVE attribution: subject of 1792 Writ of Habeas Corpus from NJ Supreme Court (in Manumission, to produce Negro Fanny). At age 22 was younger than ideal for a defendant in a complex HC matter, but the case-file's omission of any "Esq." honorific excludes Garret Janse Esq., and (age 5 in 1792) is excluded by youth. Possible third Garret cannot be ruled out.
